audit.description: Read-only audit — scan a scope, categorize findings, return structured JSON. Use proactively when the task is 'find X across files' and no writes are needed. Lower context tax than running the same search inline.

execute.description: Apply a previously-audited set of changes. Worker has write access but is constrained to a declared scope. Use when an audit produced a punch list and you want to delegate the mechanical application.

investigate.description: Read-only research + recommendation. Worker investigates a question (codebase / external / both) and returns a recommendation with reasoning. Use for 'should we / why does / where is' questions that need deeper exploration than a single grep.

plan.description: Produce a manifest + script + checklist for a change. No execution — the output is the plan, not the diff. Use when the change is large enough to want review before action.
